file_insights.purpose: Configure permissions for Claude tools and Bash commands

patterns_discovered.description: Claude uses an allowlist approach for granting permissions to tools and Bash commands
Bash permissions include claude mcp:* and sequential-thinking server setup
Settings stored in .claude/settings.local.json for project-specific configuration
Specific Bash commands can be allowed rather than blanket terminal access

gotchas_discovered.description: New .claude/settings.local.json file required for CI workflow - may not have been obvious this was needed
Permission strings must match exact format (e.g., 'Bash(claude mcp:*)') - typos will cause failures
Verifying npx command for sequential-thinking suggests dependency on specific MCP server version/availability

approach_details.execution: Created settings.local.json with required permissions for CI workflow tools
approach_details.result: CI workflow now has necessary permissions configured

recommendations.recommendation: Document each permission entry with comments explaining what it enables in the CI workflow
Clarify whether .claude/settings.local.json should be committed or added to .gitignore
Add CI checks to validate that all required permissions are present before workflow execution
Test each permission (update-config, mcp, sequential-thinking) in actual CI environment to confirm functionality
